title: The Problem with AI Agents No One is Talking About | Yutori, Abhishek Das
summary: Abhishek Das argues that most AI agents fail in practice because the industry has normalized unreliability, with compounding errors across multi-step workflows even at high per-step accuracy rates. He contends that reliability and taste in design matter more than raw performance metrics, and that proper dogfooding and proof-of-work approaches are essential for building agents that work on first deployment.
